title: Look into switching insurance — fall-2022 OSU term-startup todos
html:
TL;DR

  • Fall-quarter 2022 OSU term-startup checklist: switch insurance, dedicate one hour to a poverty assessment, do soil lab, "chilling at" a CS assignment, finalize a "pop source."
  • Title is misleading per the pattern — "Look into switching insurance" is line one, but the note is actually a mixed academic + admin term-startup stack.
  • Captures the term transition setup form: every academic transition produces an operating-environment todo (insurance, scholarship, devices) before any work-content gets written.
  • Soil lab + poverty assessment together suggest a sustainability/ag econ flavored fall-2022 schedule — consistent with Yahir's sustainability triple bottom line interest.
  • "Pop source" is genuinely ambiguous — could be a population-data source for the poverty assessment or a citation source for a paper.

Why it matters

Anchors a September-30 reset moment at OSU — the same week as the OSIG six things note. Three weeks before the 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream mega-dump kicks off. The insurance-switching line is a tiny adulthood-acquisition moment (Yahir managing his own coverage at 19) that pairs with the August "lost bank card" note as evidence of the administrative-self-management arc running underneath the founder/intellectual surface.

Open questions

  • Which insurance? (Auto, given the grants pass teen era truck context? Health? Renter's?)
  • Was the poverty assessment for an applied economics class or an extracurricular (OSIG, ELI)?
  • Did the soil lab connect to the sustainability triple bottom line thread?
